preview_text:
The model favors Brazil to win at 64%, with Scotland's chances of victory standing at 12%. The underdog faces a substantial gap, though not insurmountable in a single match.

A draw at 25% sits as a meaningful outcome in the model's distribution, suggesting competitive balance in play despite Brazil's clear edge. The 39-percentage-point spread between Brazil and Scotland reflects an asymmetric matchup where the favorite is heavily preferred but neither team is modeled as dominant enough to rule out a result other than a Brazil win.

The model and sportsbook consensus are tightly aligned across all three outcomes, each diverging by fewer than 3 percentage points. Polymarket pricing shows similar clustering. This convergence suggests broad agreement on Brazil's advantage while acknowledging realistic uncertainty about the match's true competitive character.
